name: Nemesis (Retribution)
shortDescription: Nemesis is the Greek goddess of retribution, balance, and divine justice. She punishes hubris and arrogance, ensuring that no mortal becomes too proud or escapes the consequences of their actions.
description: Nemesis, also known as Adrasteia or Rhamnousia, is the Greek goddess of retribution, divine justice, vengeance, and balance. She is the embodiment of righteous indignation against those who succumb to hubris (excessive pride) and arrogance before the gods. Nemesis ensures that mortals do not exceed their due measure and that any imbalance in the world is corrected. Her actions are not necessarily punitive in the modern sense; rather, she restores equilibrium, often by humbling the proud or bringing down those who have enjoyed undue fortune. She is sometimes described as the daughter of Nyx (Night), either alone or with Erebus (Darkness), or as the daughter of Oceanus. Nemesis's role is crucial in maintaining cosmic order. She is not simply about punishment; she is about ensuring consequences for actions and preventing excessive pride from disrupting the natural balance. In some myths, she is associated with the Trojan War, punishing Paris for his abduction of Helen and contributing to the downfall of Troy. Her symbols often include scales, swords, whips, and bridles, representing her role in measuring actions and controlling excess. The sanctuary of Nemesis at Rhamnous was particularly important, housing a famous statue of the goddess sculpted by Phidias.
